---
title: "Lab07: Machine Learning 1"
author: "Minzhou Ou (PID: A17293812)"
format: html
---

## Background

Today we will begin out exploration of some important machine learnig methods, namely **clusterring** and **dimensionnality reductionn**.

Let's make up some input data for clusterinng where we kow what the natural "clusters" are

The function `rnorm()` can be useful here.

```{r}
hist(rnorm(5000, mean=10))
```

> Q. Generate 30 random numbers centered at +3 and another 30 at -3

```{r}
temp <- c(rnorm(30, mean=3),
          rnorm(30, mean=-3))
x <- cbind(x=temp, y=rev(temp))
```

## K-means clustering
